author: @HarleyPebley
text: I enjoy both your building videos and talking videos. I don't always necessarily agree with all your points, but they're always good food for thought. Cheers and Happy New Year!

author: @mikaelfransson3658
text: I see on tele! The teletubbies on a Snl-Joke!  What a funny fars... But that I did after was a reflex on top of my soft winter hat! For me it get cleare then I see, the purple one! It look really silly on me but it does what I request I never have that idea before! Then you are near 60 you do not need to care about what other say! /Mikael

author: @grendel1960a
text: how to gain experience - be a sponge, absorb anything and everything try new things, practice old things and be prepared to fail (lots) but get back up and try again because even failure brings experience.

author: @TheShakke100
text:
Robert, I don’t comment very often, but you are truly a Mentor and inspiration to me! Please don’t downgrade yourself with / in  your new talk time videos!!
1. I can honestly say I’ve watched every video. You posted in at least the past four years.
2. I envy your dedication, knowledge, humour and variety of projects you put together so unimaginably quickly.  
3. Though my friends and family compliment me on all my passion and projects, I suffer the stress / anxiety of not always completing, pursuing, etc to it’s fullest. 
4. Videos like this and others help me realize that it’s not just me… not everything has to be completed to perfection, etc.. and I’d rather be filling my mind with new projects/ improving / helping the world and understanding than reading / watching the ‘ news’!! :)
Thank you for everything you do (and have done)!!
Mark C (Canada)
P.s. you’re NOT FAT! ;)… nor old! lol

author: @danthomas589
text: I agree; control is an illusion. I don't think you have control over your feelings but rather your reaction to your feelings. Just wanted to share my thoughts on this subject. 😄

author: @voltrevolt8731
text: Sublime advice as always — side note, Michael Faraday also learned the bookbinding trade and even set up his first lab in the corner of a bookshop…

author: @billy-go9kx
text: I believe you need to have a need and then investigate a project, at least I do.  It's like learning to change your own oil on the car.  I plan on moving in the near future and putting together an off grid solar system as a project.  I do have an electronics background so I am not intimidated.  I will also try some wind power from your videos.  The question from the viewer sounds like they want a step by step plan from you on how to be inventive.  That is not how it works.  Even collage doesn't do that.  It is dependent on the individual's character.   And I think you are a character!   LOL
